name: Pendulum Swing
description: A body swings around an anchor through a constraint, rope, chain, or joint while conserving and redirecting momentum.
design_notes: Pendulum swings need clear anchor placement, rope length, tangential input, damping, release impulse, and collision behavior. The player must understand when they are attached, how input pumps the arc, and what velocity they keep when the constraint releases.
design_purpose: Create traversal or puzzle motion based on arcs, timing, and momentum transfer.
Let players build or redirect kinetic energy instead of moving only through direct acceleration.
Support readable hazards or moving objects that obey a constrained path.
player_fantasy: The player feels momentum build through a swing and chooses the release point that carries them forward.

parameters.name: constraint_joint_id
parameters.description: Stable identifier for the joint or constraint that keeps the body attached to the anchor.

parameters.name: joint_anchor
parameters.typical_range: world position or body-local anchor point
parameters.description: Anchor position used by the rope, hinge, or distance constraint.

parameters.name: rope_length_units
parameters.description: Constraint length from anchor to body, including any authored slack or extension.

parameters.name: tangential_input_acceleration
parameters.description: Acceleration applied along the swing tangent when the player pumps or steers the arc.

parameters.name: release_impulse_multiplier
parameters.description: Multiplier applied to current tangential velocity when the body detaches from the constraint.

edge_cases: The rope anchor moves, despawns, or becomes blocked while the player is attached.
The swinging body collides with walls or ceilings and the constraint solver injects unexpected energy.
Release occurs during a rollback correction, leaving client and server with different outgoing velocity.
The rope crosses a moving platform or one-way platform and needs a rule for snagging or ignoring it.
common_bugs: Input acceleration is applied in world axes instead of along the tangent, so pumping feels inconsistent.
Constraint length changes after collision correction and slowly stretches or shrinks the rope.
Release impulse uses the previous frame velocity and drops momentum at low frame rates.
Solver iteration count is too low for long ropes, causing visible jitter near the anchor.
balancing_notes: Longer ropes create slower, wider arcs; shorter ropes demand faster release timing and tighter camera framing.
High input acceleration gives players control but can erase the pendulum feel if they can steer directly.
Release velocity should be capped or shaped when swings feed into combat, pits, or precision landings.
accessibility_notes: Provide generous attach windows, buffered release, or alternate routes when swing timing is mandatory.
Reduce camera roll or auto-camera lag during large arcs for players sensitive to sweeping motion.

implementation_notes.unity: Use HingeJoint, DistanceJoint2D, configurable joints, or a custom constraint depending on dimension and control needs. Store constraint_joint_id, anchor, rope length, and current tangential velocity; apply player input along the tangent and debug draw anchor, rope, and release vector.
implementation_notes.godot: PinJoint2D, DampedSpringJoint2D, Generic6DOFJoint3D, or custom CharacterBody constraints can model the swing. Keep rope_length_units stable after collision correction and resolve release after physics integration so outgoing velocity matches the final arc.
implementation_notes.unreal: Physics Constraint Components or custom CharacterMovement modes can own swing state. For player traversal, separate camera smoothing from physical arc calculation, replicate attach anchor and release timestamp, and validate anchors on the server.
implementation_notes.web: Matter.js constraints or a custom Verlet rope can drive browser swings. Use fixed-step integration, clamp constraint error, and store anchor id plus body velocity for rollback or replay because variable frame deltas quickly change arc height.

example_games.title: Bionic Commando
example_games.note: Uses swinging traversal built around attachment and release timing.

example_games.title: Cut the Rope
example_games.note: Uses pendulum-like motion as part of physics puzzle solving.
